title: consolida regalis subsp. arvensis
intro: Consolida regalis subsp. arvensis, also known as field forget-me-not or cornflower, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family and is characterized by its vibrant blue flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which make it beneficial for treating conditions such as digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Consolida regalis subsp. arvensis include its ability to soothe anxiety and insomnia, reduce fever, and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
